p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: High leaf density, trailing habit, consistent green pigmentation.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Stable throughout the sequence. No significant growth or decline observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_inference: Healthy; turgor pressure is maintained across all leaves.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Two primary leaves, one showing signs of chlorosis/necrosis.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Progressive browning of the leaf margins observed from T-4 to Current.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_inference: Stressed; likely due to moisture inconsistency or nutrient uptake issues.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves, one larger (apical) and one smaller (basal), rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The apical leaf has maintained a consistent orientation relative to the rabbit anchor.
p3_pothos.visual_health_inference: Stable; no signs of wilting or yellowing.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Small seedling, located near the rim of the black pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Minimal change in size; appears dormant or slow-growing.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_inference: Neutral; lacks sufficient foliage for a definitive health assessment.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently dry/friable across all pots.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds or secondary sprouts detected.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant debris or fungal growth.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The primary change across the sequence is the gradual degradation of the Mexican Mint (p2) foliage, while the Pothos (p3) and String of Nickels (p1) remain static.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is generally stable, but p2 requires intervention to prevent further necrosis.
vision_report.anomalies: None detected beyond the localized necrosis in p2.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection shows a divergence in health: the established plants (p1, p3) are thriving in their current environment, while the seedling/younger specimens (p2, p4) are struggling to adapt, evidenced by the leaf-margin necrosis in p2.
